messages.content: The AC is running but the house isn't cooling down, what should I check?

For a house resident: Let's troubleshoot this together. If your AC is running but not cooling the house effectively, the first thing to check is the air filter. Make sure it's clean and not clogged, as a dirty filter can restrict airflow and reduce the system's ability to cool. Next, take a look at the outdoor condenser coils. Check if they're dirty, covered in grass clippings, or have debris buildup, as this can insulate the coil and prevent it from rejecting heat properly. Give these a check and see if that resolves the issue. If not, we can explore further troubleshooting steps together.

retrieved_context:
Chapter 4: Cooling System Troubleshooting
If the system is running but not cooling effectively, check the air filter first, followed by inspecting the
outdoor condenser coils for dirt, grass clippings, or debris buildup, which insulate the coil and reduce its
ability to reject heat from the refrigerant.
Ice formation on the indoor evaporator coil or refrigerant lines usually indicates restricted airflow or low
refrigerant charge; turn off cooling and run the fan only until the ice melts before further diagnosis, since
running the compressor with an iced coil can cause liquid refrigerant to return to the compressor and
damage it.
If the outdoor fan is not spinning while the compressor runs, this may indicate a failed fan motor or

performance changes to help technicians diagnose recurring issues more quickly, since intermittent
problems are often easier to trace with a documented history than a single snapshot inspection.
Before the cooling season begins, run the system in cooling mode briefly even if the weather is still
mild, to confirm the compressor engages properly and cold air is produced, giving time to schedule a
repair before hot weather makes the home uncomfortable without air conditioning.
Before the heating season begins, similarly test the furnace or heat pump's heating function, and
replace the thermostat batteries and air filter together as a seasonal routine to reduce the chance of a
mid-winter service call.

to sense a temperature unrepresentative of the room as a whole.
If cooling capacity seems adequate on mild days but insufficient during extreme heat, this may simply
reflect the system operating at its rated capacity limit rather than indicating a fault; consult the sizing
worksheet in Appendix C to confirm the unit was sized appropriately for your home's square footage
and insulation level.

capacitor, which requires a licensed HVAC technician, since handling refrigerant components and
high-voltage capacitors safely requires specialized tools and training.
Error code C4 on the thermostat indicates the outdoor unit is reporting a high pressure fault, typically
caused by restricted outdoor airflow or an overcharged refrigerant circuit. Error code C7 indicates a
communication failure between the thermostat and outdoor unit, often resolved by checking low-voltage
wiring connections at both ends.
If the system short-cycles during cooling calls (turning on and off in rapid succession), check that the
thermostat is not located near a supply vent or direct sunlight, both of which can cause the thermostat
to sense a temperature unrepresentative of the room as a whole.
